rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports and data types.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees, avoiding implementation-specific references.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE contracts are syntactically valid and use only visible symbols from the AADL model.
Define assumptions and guarantees using concrete bounds or named constants when available.
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Align helper declaration types with the actual expression types (bool, int, real).
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference valid AADL component features and ports.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the data type of the referenced port or variable.
Do not include AADL property names like Compute_Execution_Time as AGREE variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables.
Do not generate clauses for Boolean data ports without explicit numeric scalar representation.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless typed ports or constants are exposed.
irrelevant_topics: AADL model fusion and validation repair patterns
